name: coreutils
title: coreutils
summary: test - check file types and compare values
description:
GNU core utilities
This package contains the basic file, shell and text manipulation
utilities which are expected to exist on every operating system.
.
Specifically, this package includes:
arch base64 basename cat chcon chgrp chmod chown chroot cksum comm cp
csplit cut date dd df dir dircolors dirname du echo env expand expr
factor false flock fmt fold groups head hostid id install join link ln
logname ls md5sum mkdir mkfifo mknod mktemp mv nice nl nohup nproc numfmt
od paste pathchk pinky pr printenv printf ptx pwd readlink realpath rm
rmdir runcon sha*sum seq shred sleep sort split stat stty sum sync tac
tail tee test timeout touch tr true truncate tsort tty uname unexpand
uniq unlink users vdir wc who whoami yes

commands.description: check file types and compare values

commands.name: arch
commands.description: print machine hardware name (same as uname -m)

commands.name: b2sum
commands.description: compute and check BLAKE2 message digest

commands.name: base32
commands.description: base32 encode/decode data and print to standard output

commands.name: base64
commands.description: base64 encode/decode data and print to standard output

commands.name: basename
commands.description: strip directory and suffix from filenames

commands.name: basenc
commands.description: Encode/decode data and print to standard output

commands.name: cat
commands.description: concatenate files and print on the standard output

commands.name: chcon
commands.description: change file security context

commands.name: chgrp
commands.description: change group ownership

commands.name: chmod
commands.description: change file mode bits

commands.name: chown
commands.description: change file owner and group

commands.name: cksum
commands.description: compute and verify file checksums

commands.name: comm
commands.description: compare two sorted files line by line

commands.name: cp
commands.description: copy files and directories

commands.name: csplit
commands.description: split a file into sections determined by context lines

commands.name: cut
commands.description: remove sections from each line of files

commands.name: date
commands.description: print or set the system date and time

commands.name: dd
commands.description: convert and copy a file

commands.name: df
commands.description: report file system space usage

commands.name: dir
commands.description: list directory contents

commands.name: dircolors
commands.description: color setup for ls

commands.name: dirname
commands.description: strip last component from file name

commands.name: du
commands.description: estimate file space usage

commands.name: echo
commands.description: display a line of text

commands.name: env
commands.description: run a program in a modified environment

commands.name: expand
commands.description: convert tabs to spaces

commands.name: expr
commands.description: evaluate expressions

commands.name: factor
commands.description: factor numbers

commands.name: false
commands.description: do nothing, unsuccessfully

commands.name: fmt
commands.description: simple optimal text formatter

commands.name: fold
commands.description: wrap each input line to fit in specified width

commands.name: groups
commands.description: print the groups a user is in

commands.name: head
commands.description: Simple command line user agent

commands.name: hostid
commands.description: print the numeric identifier for the current host

commands.name: id
commands.description: print real and effective user and group IDs

commands.name: install
commands.description: copy files and set attributes

commands.name: join
commands.description: join lines of two files on a common field

commands.name: link
commands.description: call the link function to create a link to a file

commands.name: ln
commands.description: make links between files

commands.name: logname
commands.description: print user's login name

commands.name: ls
commands.description: list directory contents

commands.name: md5sum
commands.description: compute and check MD5 message digest

commands.name: mkdir
commands.description: make directories

commands.name: mkfifo
commands.description: make FIFOs (named pipes)

commands.name: mknod
commands.description: make block or character special files

commands.name: mktemp
commands.description: create a temporary file or directory

commands.name: mv
commands.description: move (rename) files

commands.name: nice
commands.description: run a program with modified scheduling priority

commands.name: nl
commands.description: number lines of files

commands.name: nohup
commands.description: run a command immune to hangups, with output to a non-tty

commands.name: nproc
commands.description: print the number of processing units available

commands.name: numfmt
commands.description: Convert numbers from/to human-readable strings

commands.name: od
commands.description: dump files in octal and other formats

commands.name: paste

commands.name: pinky
commands.description: lightweight finger

commands.name: printf
commands.description: format and print data

commands.name: ptx
commands.description: produce a permuted index of file contents

commands.name: pwd
commands.description: print name of current/working directory

commands.name: readlink
commands.description: print resolved symbolic links or canonical file names

commands.name: realpath
commands.description: print the resolved path

commands.name: rm
commands.description: remove files or directories

commands.name: rmdir
commands.description: remove empty directories

commands.name: runcon
commands.description: run command with specified security context

commands.name: seq
commands.description: print a sequence of numbers

commands.description: compute and check SHA1 message digest

commands.description: compute and check SHA224 message digest

commands.description: compute and check SHA384 message digest

commands.description: compute and check SHA512 message digest

commands.name: shred
commands.description: overwrite a file to hide its contents, and optionally delete it

commands.name: shuf
commands.description: generate random permutations

commands.name: sleep
commands.description: delay for a specified amount of time

commands.name: sort
commands.description: sort lines of text files

commands.name: split
commands.description: split a file into pieces

commands.name: stat
commands.description: display file or file system status

commands.name: stdbuf
commands.description: Run COMMAND, with modified buffering operations for its standard streams.

commands.name: stty
commands.description: change and print terminal line settings

commands.name: sum
commands.description: checksum and count the blocks in a file

commands.name: sync
commands.description: Synchronize cached writes to persistent storage

commands.name: tac
commands.description: concatenate and print files in reverse

commands.name: tail
commands.description: output the last part of files

commands.name: tee
commands.description: read from standard input and write to standard output and files

commands.name: test
commands.description: check file types and compare values

commands.name: timeout
commands.description: run a command with a time limit

commands.name: touch
commands.description: create file, or change file timestamps

commands.name: tr
commands.description: translate or delete characters

commands.name: true
commands.description: do nothing, successfully

commands.name: truncate
commands.description: empty a table or set of tables

commands.name: tsort
commands.description: perform topological sort

commands.name: tty
commands.description: print the file name of the terminal connected to standard input

commands.name: uname
commands.description: print system information

commands.name: unexpand
commands.description: convert spaces to tabs

commands.name: uniq
commands.description: report or omit repeated lines

commands.name: unlink
commands.description: call the unlink function to remove the specified file

commands.name: users
commands.description: print the user names of users currently logged in to the current host

commands.name: vdir
commands.description: list directory contents

commands.name: wc
commands.description: print newline, word, and byte counts for each file

commands.name: who
commands.description: show who is logged on

commands.name: whoami
commands.description: print effective user name

commands.name: yes
commands.description: output a string repeatedly until killed

commands.name: chroot
commands.description: change root directory

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use [ because test - check file types and compare values.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use [ as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `[ EXPRESSION ]` as documented upstream for [.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `[ ]` as documented upstream for [.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `[ OPTION` as documented upstream for [.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair [ with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `[ --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install coreutils. test - check file types and compare values.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: test - check file types and compare values.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: test - check file types and compare values.
